model.description: MiMo • The flagship foundation for the agentic era, built for complex, long-horizon, multi-step agentic tasks.

pick.rationale: While St. John's holds a slight analytical edge with superior KenPom (20 vs 21) and Torvik (18 vs 22) rankings and a better 27-6 record, Kansas's elite defensive efficiency (rank 10) and tournament experience under Bill Self provide crucial advantages. The Jayhawks' ability to control tempo and get key stops against St. John's slightly better offensive efficiency (rank 47 vs 57) could prove decisive in a tight matchup. Despite St. John's momentum from winning the Big East tournament, Kansas's defensive prowess and experience in close games give them the edge in this evenly matched 4/5 contest.

reasoningStep.title: St. John's vs Kansas Round of 32 Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Both teams are similarly ranked with Kansas having a defensive edge while St. John's has better overall metrics and recent momentum.
reasoningStep.evidence: KenPom rankings: St. John's 20, Kansas 21. Torvik rankings: St. John's 18, Kansas 22. Defensive efficiency: Kansas rank 10, St. John's rank 12. Offensive efficiency: St. John's rank 47, Kansas rank 57. Records: St. John's 27-6, Kansas 23-10. St. John's won Big East tournament with six straight wins including over UConn. Kansas has elite defensive efficiency and tournament experience under Bill Self.

By Shreyas Laddha March 15, 2026 7:28 PM Key Takeaways Key Takeaways AI-generated summary reviewed by our newsroom. Read our AI Policy. KU drew a No. 4 seed in the East, opening against No. 13 Cal Baptist in San Diego. Potential path pits Kansas against strong opponents like St. John’s and Duke. KU’s tournament outlook hinges on offensive recovery and Darryn Peterson’s play. LAWRENCE The Kansas Jayhawks men’s basketball team learned its assignment for the NCAA Tournament on Sunday evening, drawing a No. 4 seed in the East Regional. Duke is the No. 1 seed, followed by UConn at No. 2, Michigan State at No. 3, Kansas at No. 4, St. John’s at No. 5 and Louisville at No. 6. Here’s a look at the Jayhawks’ potential path to the 2026 Final Four and whether they received lucky or unlucky draws along the way: Round of 64 KU’s first-round opponent: No. 13 seed Cal Baptist.
